ingredients.entityContext: This oceanfront hotel in Ginowan appears in episode 8 as a location the characters visit or pass through, reflecting the modern resort landscape along Okinawa's western coast.

ingredients.name: Hamachidori Monument
ingredients.entityContext: This stone monument inscribed with the Okinawan folk song 'Hamachidori' appears in episode 10 as a quiet contemplative location where a character reflects on Okinawan heritage and belonging.

ingredients.address: Kanehide Gushikawa Branch, 4 Chome-2-6 Midorimachi, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2215, Japan

ingredients.address: Japan, 〒904-2223 Okinawa, Uruma, Gushikawa, 3231-1 Smilax Kurichi

ingredients.name: Tsuha Shamisen Shop
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒904-2221 Okinawa, Uruma, Tairagawa, 184-1 津波三味線店
ingredients.entityContext: This real shamisen instrument shop in Uruma is featured in episode 12 as a nod to Okinawan musical culture, with a character stopping outside it in a scene that underscores the show's celebration of local traditions.

ingredients.localName: 田原コンクリート工業

ingredients.name: Gushikawa Coast
ingredients.address: Gushikawa Beach, Gushikawa, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2223, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Gushikawa beach backdrop appears in episode 2 as the place where the group first hangs out together, capturing the carefree Okinawan summer atmosphere central to the show's tone. The rocky shoreline and turquoise water match the anime's iconic establishing shots.

ingredients.name: Sun Rise Cafe para para
ingredients.localName: Sun Rise Cafe パラパラ
ingredients.entityContext: This small cafe near the Gushikawa coast appears in episode 1 as a local hangout spot the characters visit, grounding the story in the everyday commercial life of Uruma City.

ingredients.name: Teruma Beach
ingredients.address: 8VPV+FR Teruma Beach, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2301, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Teruma Beach appears in episode 1 as one of the first scenic Okinawan locations shown, establishing the series' sun-drenched island setting and introducing the summer break mood.

ingredients.name: Bise-Fukugi Tree Road
ingredients.address: Bise-Fukugi Tree Road, 389 Bise, Motobu, Kunigami District, Okinawa 905-0207, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic fukugi tree-lined road in Bise village, Motobu, is featured in the ending credits sequence, representing the lush natural scenery of Okinawa that frames the anime's pastoral mood.

ingredients.name: Tsuhakogumi Corporation
ingredients.localName: 株式会社 津波古組

ingredients.name: Okinawa Rail Observation Deck
ingredients.localName: ヤンバルクイナ展望台
ingredients.address: Okinawa Rail Observation Deck, Hedo, Kunigami, Kunigami District, Okinawa 905-1421, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Yanbaru Kuina observation deck at Cape Hedo, Okinawa's northernmost tip, appears in the ending credits, evoking the full geographic sweep of the island the characters call home.

ingredients.name: Kouri Bridge View Point
ingredients.localName: 古宇利大橋 南詰展望所
ingredients.address: Kouri Bridge South View Point, Sumuide, Nago, Okinawa 905-1635, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The scenic viewpoint at the base of Kouri Bridge, with its sweeping ocean panorama, appears in the ending credits sequence as one of Okinawa's most picturesque landmarks woven into the show's visual identity.

ingredients.name: Kafu Banta
ingredients.address: Kafu Banta, 2768 Yonashiromiyagi, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2423, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: This dramatic sea cliff viewpoint on the Katsuren Peninsula is featured in the ending credits, representing the rugged coastal beauty of Uruma that forms the anime's home territory.

ingredients.name: Shureimon Gate
ingredients.address: Shureimon Gate, 1 Chome-7-1 Shurikinjocho, Naha, Okinawa 903-0826, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The iconic Shureimon gate of Shuri Castle in Naha appears in the ending credits sequence, symbolizing Okinawa's deeper historical and cultural identity beneath the slice-of-life story.

ingredients.name: Tomori Stone Lion
ingredients.address: Tomori Stone Lion, 22 Tomori, Yaese, Shimajiri District, Okinawa 901-0402, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The Tomori stone shisa lion, one of Okinawa's oldest and most revered guardian figures, appears in the ending credits as a symbol of the island's traditional spiritual landscape.

ingredients.name: Heart Rock
ingredients.entityContext: The famous heart-shaped rock formation on Kouri Island appears in the ending credits, used as a symbol of the romantic and scenic backdrop of Okinawa that the series evokes.

ingredients.name: First-class architect office e-craft.
ingredients.localName: 一級建築士事務所 e-craft.

ingredients.name: A&W Makiminato
ingredients.address: A&W Makiminato, 4 Chome-9-1 Makiminato, Urasoe, Okinawa 901-2131, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The A&W fast food restaurant in Makiminato, a beloved Okinawa-only chain with American roots, appears in episode 8 as a place the characters eat, emphasizing the unique American-influenced food culture of Okinawa.

ingredients.address: Shuri Station, 3-chōme Shuriterachō, Naha, Okinawa 903-0806, Japan

ingredients.address: Mid-Sea Road Beach, Yonashiroyahira, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2427, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The beach along Kaichu-doro, the road that connects the island chain east of Okinawa, appears in episode 8 as a scenic driving destination the characters visit, highlighting one of Uruma's most distinctive local landmarks.

ingredients.name: Gushikawa Community Center
ingredients.entityContext: The Gushikawa public community hall appears in episode 5 as a location for a local community event scene, illustrating the tight-knit neighborhood life in the Gushikawa area of Uruma.

ingredients.localName: サンエー 具志川メインシティ
ingredients.address: San-A Gushikawa Main City, 450ｰ1 Esu, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2244, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: San-A's flagship Gushikawa shopping mall appears in episode 5 as a central commercial hub where characters run errands, capturing the everyday suburban life of Uruma City that the anime depicts with affection.

ingredients.name: Banshoato Park
ingredients.entityContext: This small park on the historic site of a former checkpost appears in episode 4 as a quiet spot the characters visit in the Gushikawa neighborhood, tying everyday life to the area's historical layering.

ingredients.name: Yonashiro Athletic Park
ingredients.address: Yonashiro Athletic Park, 4 Yonashirochuo, Uruma, Okinawa 904-2305, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: Yonashiro's multipurpose park appears in episode 4 as an outdoor activity setting for the characters, reflecting the public green spaces of eastern Uruma where much of the story takes place.

ingredients.name: Okitsura Bench
ingredients.entityContext: This seafront bench on the Gushikawa coast serves as the recurring gathering spot where the main characters meet, talk, and watch the ocean, making it the emotional anchor of the series. Fans treat it as the definitive pilgrimage stop for the anime.
